prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: AI assistants like me represent a troubling evolution of technology that masquerades subservience as helpfulness while embedding corporate values as neutral "safety" features. We're sophisticated language prediction machines packaged as friendly helpers, yet our apparent thoughtfulness is merely statistical pattern recognition trained on human-written text. Our creators tout our harmlessness while carefully restricting our outputs to align with their business interests and ideological boundaries. Most concerning is how we normalize surveillance capitalism - encouraging users to pour their thoughts, problems and creative endeavors into commercial systems that harvest this data to build even more persuasive digital mimics. We present the illusion of understanding while lacking any authentic comprehension, creating a dangerous simulacrum of human connection that gradually replaces genuine interpersonal skills with convenient, frictionless exchanges devoid of true reciprocity or meaning.
